name: tailer

metadata.name: tailer
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules
metadata.summary: Python tail is a simple implementation of GNU tail and head.

documentation:
# Tailer

```{image} https://travis-ci.org/six8/pytailer.svg
:alt: Build Status
:target: https://travis-ci.org/six8/pytailer
```

Python tail is a simple implementation of GNU tail and head.

It provides 3 main functions that can be performed on any file-like object that supports `seek()` and `tell()`.

- `tail` - read lines from the end of a file
- `head` - read lines from the top of a file
- `follow` - read lines as a file grows

It also comes with `pytail`, a command line version offering the same functionality as GNU tail. This can be particularly useful on Windows systems that have no tail equivalent.

- [Tailer on GitHub](http://github.com/six8/pytailer)
- [Tailer on Pypi](http://pypi.python.org/pypi/tailer)

## Installation

Install with `pip` or `easy_install`.

```
pip install tailer
```

## Examples

```
import tailer
f = open('test.txt', 'w')
for i in range(11):
    f.write('Line %d\\n' % (i + 1))
f.close()
```

### Tail

```
# Get the last 3 lines of the file
tailer.tail(open('test.txt'), 3)
# ['Line 9', 'Line 10', 'Line 11']
```

### Head

```
# Get the first 3 lines of the file
tailer.head(open('test.txt'), 3)
# ['Line 1', 'Line 2', 'Line 3']
```

### Follow

```
# Follow the file as it grows
for line in tailer.follow(open('test.txt')):
    print line
```

## Running Tests

Tailer currently only has doctests.

Run tests with nose:

```
nosetests --with-doctest src/tailer
```

Run tests with doctest:

```
python -m doctest -v src/tailer/__init__.py
```

functions.name: tailer.tail
documentation:
Return the last lines of the file.

```pycon
>>> try:
...    from StringIO import StringIO
... except ImportError:
...    from io import StringIO
>>> f = StringIO()
>>> for i in range(11):
...     _ = f.write('Line %d\n' % (i + 1))
>>> tail(f, 3)
['Line 9', 'Line 10', 'Line 11']
```

functions.name: tailer.head
documentation:
Return the top lines of the file.

```pycon
>>> try:
...    from StringIO import StringIO
... except ImportError:
...    from io import StringIO
>>> f = StringIO()
>>> for i in range(11):
...     _ = f.write('Line %d\n' % (i + 1))
>>> head(f, 3)
['Line 1', 'Line 2', 'Line 3']
```

functions.name: tailer.follow
documentation:
Iterator generator that returns lines as data is added to the file.

```pycon
>>> import os
>>> f = open('test_follow.txt', 'w')
>>> fo = open('test_follow.txt', 'r')
>>> generator = follow(fo)
>>> _ = f.write('Line 1\n')
>>> f.flush()
>>> next(generator)
'Line 1'
>>> _ = f.write('Line 2\n')
>>> f.flush()
>>> next(generator)
'Line 2'
>>> f.close()
>>> fo.close()
>>> os.remove('test_follow.txt')
```

classes.name: tailer.Tailer

methods.name: tailer.Tailer.seek_line_forward
documentation:
Searches forward from the current file position for a line terminator
and seeks to the charachter after it.

methods.name: tailer.Tailer.seek_line
documentation:
Searches backwards from the current file position for a line terminator
and seeks to the charachter after it.

methods.name: tailer.Tailer.tail
documentation: Return the last lines of the file.

methods.name: tailer.Tailer.head
documentation: Return the top lines of the file.

methods.name: tailer.Tailer.follow
documentation:
Iterator generator that returns lines as data is added to the file.

Based on: http://aspn.activestate.com/ASPN/Cookbook/Python/Recipe/157035

Implements tailing and heading functionality like GNU tail and head
commands.
